hero.title: SolarX: AI-Powered Battery Optimization
hero.subtitle:
Compare Samsung, LG, and Tesla batteries for solar-powered humanoid robot charging stations
using LSTM prediction and physics-informed models.

story.title: 📖 Story
story.description: Project evolution from v1.0 to v5.0

prediction.mapeNote: MAPE in the 60% range is typical for solar generation prediction with weather variability

story.problemText1: Humanoid robots require efficient, cost-effective charging infrastructure. Traditional grid-only solutions face high electricity costs during peak hours. Solar + ESS (Energy Storage System) combinations promise savings, but which battery vendor offers the best ROI?
story.problemText2: This project compares three global battery manufacturers—LG Energy Solution, Samsung SDI, and Tesla—using real-world data from Korean solar farms and power exchange (SMP) pricing.
story.solutionItem1Desc: Predicts solar generation 24 hours ahead using weather data (temperature, humidity, solar radiation, cloud cover)
story.solutionItem2Desc: Simulates real battery behavior including C-rate limits, temperature effects, efficiency losses, and degradation (SOH tracking)
story.solutionItem3Desc: Calculates CAPEX, OPEX, ROI, payback period, and NPV using real Korean SMP (System Marginal Price) data
story.v1AchievementText: Basic battery charge/discharge simulation with dummy data
story.v1MetricsText: No prediction, manual price thresholds, constant efficiency
story.v2AchievementText: Trained LSTM on real solar farm data from Donghae, Korea
story.v3AchievementText: Added C-rate limits, SOC constraints, temperature-dependent efficiency
story.v3LearningText: Battery degradation (SOH) is critical for long-term economics
story.v3MetricsText: SOH tracking implemented, cycle counting added
story.v4AchievementText: Full CAPEX/OPEX modeling with vendor-specific costs
story.v4LearningText: Samsung SDI won despite lower efficiency due to better degradation rate
story.v5AchievementText: React + FastAPI web interface with interactive demos
story.v5LearningText: Portfolio presentation requires both technical depth and UX polish
story.v5MetricsText: 100% test coverage, Docker deployment, <5s simulation runtime
story.insight1Text: Samsung won despite 98.5% efficiency (vs Tesla's 97%) because of superior degradation resistance (0.00009 vs 0.0001 per cycle)
story.insight2Text: LG's 2.0C allows faster charging but causes more stress. Samsung's balanced 1.8C proved optimal for longevity.
story.insight3Text: 10× improvement over random baseline (MAE: 53.78 kW vs ~500 kW random guessing)
